summary: Make a strict active installed-slot pointer and lifecycle lease the only production-unreachable sidecar spawn capability.
rationale: Issue #366 adds one bounded installed-release boundary: a canonical active pointer selects a concrete A/B position, a non-copyable live lease holds that slot through direct-child reap, and native CI proves real cooperative lock conflict on every existing Windows and macOS launch-binding host. It preserves production caller count zero and does not add packaging, runtime, browser, provider, CLI, handshake, or remote-service composition.
verification: uv run ruff check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check scripts/build_offline_macos_intel.py tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_build_offline_macos_intel.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_installed_slot_lease.py tests/test_owned_sidecar_process.py tests/test_installed_release_admission.py tests/test_native_launch_binding_probe.py -q
uv run --group dev ruff check src tests experiments tools
uv run --group dev ty check src tests tools
uv run --group dev python tools/check_privacy_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_agent_safety_gate.py --base origin/main
uv run --group dev python tools/check_ai_bad_smells.py --base origin/main
deletion_targets: spawn paths that accept a bare authenticated admission instead of a live installed-slot lease
the claim that bounded physical A/B positions are durable slot identities
native workflow evidence that omits the active-slot lease contract
risks: The lease is a cooperative lifecycle boundary; it does not claim macOS writable-slot protection against a non-cooperating same-UID process.

Windows full-chain share-deny launch binding, macOS codesign/authenticity, a packaged sidecar, suspended child evidence, handshake, and authenticated history admission remain separate production-unreachable slices.
No runtime, provider, browser, CLI, configuration, or production caller imports this boundary.
